title: rosa × dumetorum subsp. subglabra
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subsp. subglabra, also known as the subglabrous wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its dense, prickly shrubs with fragrant, white or pink flowers. The subglabrous wild rose has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Rosa × dumetorum subsp. subglabra can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
